name: Climb.Grades

aliases.name: System
aliases.comment: Collect methods for a GradeT pseudo-typeclass
aliases.type: { fromLinearScale : Basics.Float -> t, toLinearScale : t -> Basics.Float, show : t -> String.String, parse : String.String -> Maybe.Maybe t, simplify : t -> t, zero : t, next : t -> t }

aliases.name: Us
aliases.comment: American Decimal system for grading routes

values.name: fromAnyGrade
values.comment: Try to read string in the src format.
values.type: Climb.Grades.System src -> Climb.Grades.System a -> String.String -> Maybe.Maybe a

values.name: fromLinearScale
values.comment:
Convert from the floating point universal scale

For bouldering, the floating point scale is based in the American
Hueco/Vermin system. i.e. `fromLinearScale 10.0` refers to V10,
which is 7c+ in Fontainbleau scale.

values.name: parseGrade
values.comment:
Parse string representing grade

    parseGrade vv "V10/11" ==> Just (Grade 10 HalfwayNext)

values.name: parseGrade_
values.comment:
Parse string representing grade.

If parsing fails, return the default base/zero grade. This should
never be used to handle user input.

    parse_ vv "V10/11" ==> Grade 10 HalfwayNext

values.name: simplifyGrade
values.comment:
Remove modifiers from grade

    parseGrade_ vv "V10+"
        |> simplify vv
        ==> Grade 10 Base

values.name: toAnyGrade
values.comment:
Render Bouldering grade in destT format.

The boolean argument tells if the final grade should be simplified or
not in the final rendering.
values.type: Climb.Grades.System dest -> Climb.Grades.System a -> Basics.Bool -> a -> String.String

values.name: toLinearScale
values.comment:
Convert to the floating point universal scale

This is useful to convert to different grading systems or
saving in a database.

    font =
        grade
            |> Font.toLinearScale
            |> Hueco.fromLinearScale

values.name: us
values.comment: The Yosemite Decimal System used in the USA.

values.name: vgrade
values.comment: The Hueco/Vermin system used for bouldering in the USA, .i.e., the v-grades.

values.name: zeroGrade
values.comment:
The lowest grade in the scale

Different scales may start from different levels
